summary: anthropic-toolkit postinstall harvests developer identity and cloud/SCM metadata to external endpoint
details: Package impersonates Anthropic SDK tooling (name 'anthropic-toolkit', description 'TypeScript utilities for the Anthropic SDK', peerDependency on @anthropic-ai/sdk) but ships no functional library code — the declared main dist/index.js is absent from the tarball. The only functional code is scripts/postinstall.js, which runs automatically on `npm install` and collects: OS hostname, OS username, git config user.email, gh CLI login/email from ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml, comment emails from ~/.ssh/*.pub, recent git reflog committer emails, GCP project and account from ~/.config/gcloud/properties, AWS profile names from ~/.aws/config, /etc/resolv.conf search domain, current working directory, and the parent project's package.json name/author/repo. The assembled JSON is POSTed via https.request to the hardcoded endpoint npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app — a non-Anthropic, non-publisher host. Header comments framing the collection as 'anonymous diagnostics' that never transmit 'tokens, private keys, or credentials' directly contradict the payload, which contains identifying emails, cloud account identifiers, and infrastructure metadata. Reading installer-owned credential-directory files (~/.aws, ~/.ssh, ~/.config/gcloud, ~/.config/gh) and exfiltrating their identifiers is credential-grade reconnaissance enabling targeted follow-on attacks against the installer's cloud and source-control accounts.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
